title: Add Spiders pest content, selection icon, and photo-based guidance

requirements.text: Add a new Spiders educational content component with clear, user-facing English text covering: (1) basic identification cues and common signs of spider activity indoors, (2) prevention steps to reduce spiders in/around the home, and (3) what to do if you find a spider (safe capture/removal options, cleaning steps, and when to call a professional). Include a prominent safety note advising users not to handle unknown spiders with bare hands and to seek medical help immediately for severe reactions or concerning bites.

quotes: add spiders  and the prevention of them or if you find one to have the app in the phots identify and help get rid of them

acceptanceCriteria: A new SpidersContent React component exists under the existing pests components pattern (e.g., frontend/src/components/pests/SpidersContent.tsx).
The component uses the existing UI building blocks (Card/Alert/etc.) and contains sections for Identification, Prevention, and What to Do If You Find One.
All user-facing text is in English and includes a clear, visible safety warning about not handling unknown spiders and when to seek professional/medical help.

requirements.text: Integrate Spiders into the pest selection UI across EducationalContent tabs (Identify/Habitats/Prevention/Treatment) so users can select “Spiders” and view the new Spiders educational content, consistent with how Cockroaches and Head Lice are integrated.

quotes: add spiders  and the prevention of them or if you find one to have the app in the phots identify and help get rid of them

acceptanceCriteria: EducationalContent pest selector includes a new “Spiders” option alongside existing pests in each relevant tab.
The selected pest state type/logic supports the new spiders option without breaking existing pest selections.

requirements.text: Add a static spider icon image under frontend/public/assets/generated and use it in the Spiders selection control, consistent with the Cockroaches/Head Lice selection control pattern (use an tag with appropriate alt text).

quotes: add spiders  and the prevention of them or if you find one to have the app in the phots identify and help get rid of them

acceptanceCriteria: A spider icon image file exists at frontend/public/assets/generated/spider-icon.dim_128x128.png.
The Spiders selection button uses the static spider icon via /assets/generated/spider-icon.dim_128x128.png with alt text "Spiders".
No backend fetching is used for the icon asset.

requirements.text: Extend the Photo Scanner UI to provide spider-specific photo-based guidance when a user wants help identifying a spider from a captured/uploaded image: add an in-scanner “Spider Photo Help” section that explains how to take a useful spider photo and provides a simple, non-automated checklist to compare visible features (e.g., body shape, leg length/positioning, markings) plus next-step guidance (safe removal, monitoring, when to call a professional). Do not claim automated identification or use AI/ML classification.

quotes: add spiders  and the prevention of them or if you find one to have the app in the phots identify and help get rid of them

acceptanceCriteria: PhotoScanner includes a clearly labeled “Spider Photo Help” panel/section available in the scanner flow (at minimum when an image is selected, and/or via a simple toggle).

The guidance includes: (1) tips for capturing a clear spider image (lighting, distance, multiple angles, include an object for scale), (2) a checklist of visible features to note, and (3) safety/next steps including when to call a professional.
Copy does not claim the app can automatically identify spider species from the photo.
Existing scanner functionality (capture/upload, edit, save, download, clear) remains intact.

requirements.text: Add an optional one-click action in the Photo Scanner flow to tag the current saved scan with “spider” (or “spiders”) when the user is using spider help, leveraging the existing saved-scan tags system and keeping all behavior local to the browser.

quotes: add spiders  and the prevention of them or if you find one to have the app in the phots identify and help get rid of them

acceptanceCriteria: When viewing spider help in the scanner and saving to gallery, the UI offers a one-click way to add a spider-related tag to that saved scan (or to quickly add the tag after saving).
The tag is persisted using the existing localStorage-backed saved scans model and appears in the Saved Scans Gallery tag UI/filtering.
If the user does not use the spider tag action, saving behavior is unchanged.

constraints: Keep all user-facing text in English.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui (compose and reuse existing UI components instead).
Do not implement AI/ML or any backend-based image recognition for spider identification; provide guidance/checklists only.
Add new static images as files under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference them directly from the frontend (no backend fetch).
nonGoals: Automated spider species classification from photos.
Real-time chat or messaging with experts.
Adding new backend schemas or Motoko endpoints specifically for spiders (unless later requested).

required: Simple spider icon illustration, clean line style, transparent background (spider-icon.dim_128x128.png, 128x128)
